OP_FRAME_CNOT.summary: CNOT update on the Heisenberg tracking frame.
OP_FRAME_CNOT.detail: Applies a CNOT (controlled-X) conjugation to the virtual Pauli frame U_C. This is pure bookkeeping with no state vector work -- the frame tracks how Clifford gates transform the Pauli basis.

OP_FRAME_CZ.summary: CZ update on the Heisenberg tracking frame.
OP_FRAME_CZ.detail: Applies a controlled-Z conjugation to the virtual Pauli frame U_C. Like all frame ops, this has zero cost on the state vector -- it only updates the algebraic tracking structure.

OP_FRAME_H.summary: Hadamard update on the Heisenberg tracking frame.
OP_FRAME_H.detail: Applies a Hadamard conjugation to a single virtual axis in the Pauli frame. Swaps X and Z components of that axis in the frame.

OP_FRAME_S.summary: S-gate update on the Heisenberg tracking frame.
OP_FRAME_S.detail: Applies an S (pi/4 phase) conjugation to a single virtual axis in the Pauli frame. Maps X -> Y on that axis.

OP_FRAME_S_DAG.summary: S-dagger update on the Heisenberg tracking frame.
OP_FRAME_S_DAG.detail: Applies an S-dagger (inverse pi/4 phase) conjugation to a single virtual axis in the Pauli frame. Maps X -> -Y on that axis.

OP_FRAME_SWAP.summary: SWAP update on the Heisenberg tracking frame.
OP_FRAME_SWAP.detail: Swaps two virtual axes in the Pauli frame. Used by the compiler to route qubits for measurement or gate decomposition.

OP_ARRAY_CNOT.summary: CNOT gate on the Schrodinger state vector.
OP_ARRAY_CNOT.detail: Applies a controlled-X gate to two active virtual axes in the state vector |phi>_A. Pairs of amplitudes separated by the target axis stride are XOR-permuted.

OP_ARRAY_CZ.summary: CZ gate on the Schrodinger state vector.
OP_ARRAY_CZ.detail: Applies a controlled-Z gate to two active virtual axes. Negates amplitudes where both axes are in the |1> state.

OP_ARRAY_SWAP.summary: SWAP gate on the Schrodinger state vector.
OP_ARRAY_SWAP.detail: Swaps amplitudes between two active virtual axes. Used to route the measurement target to the highest axis before collapse.

OP_ARRAY_H.summary: Hadamard gate on the Schrodinger state vector.
OP_ARRAY_H.detail: Applies a Hadamard (butterfly) transform to one active virtual axis. Each pair of amplitudes is replaced by their sum and difference, divided by sqrt(2).

OP_ARRAY_S.summary: S-gate (phase) on the Schrodinger state vector.
OP_ARRAY_S.detail: Applies diag(1, i) to one active virtual axis. Multiplies all amplitudes where that axis is |1> by i.

OP_ARRAY_S_DAG.summary: S-dagger gate on the Schrodinger state vector.
OP_ARRAY_S_DAG.detail: Applies diag(1, -i) to one active virtual axis. Multiplies all amplitudes where that axis is |1> by -i.

OP_EXPAND.summary: Promote a dormant qubit to active, growing k by 1.
OP_EXPAND.detail: Doubles the state vector by applying a virtual Hadamard on a dormant axis: k -> k+1 and gamma is divided by sqrt(2). The new axis is initialized to equal superposition. This is the only instruction that grows the active subspace.

OP_PHASE_T.summary: T-gate (pi/8 phase rotation) on an active axis.
OP_PHASE_T.detail: Applies diag(1, exp(i*pi/4)) to one active virtual axis. This is the primary non-Clifford gate -- it cannot be absorbed into the Heisenberg frame and requires direct state vector work.

OP_PHASE_T_DAG.summary: T-dagger (inverse pi/8 phase rotation) on an active axis.
OP_PHASE_T_DAG.detail: Applies diag(1, exp(-i*pi/4)) to one active virtual axis. The conjugate of OP_PHASE_T.

OP_MEAS_DORMANT_STATIC.summary: Deterministic measurement of a dormant qubit.
OP_MEAS_DORMANT_STATIC.detail: The measured Pauli observable has a fixed eigenvalue determined by the Pauli frame vector p_x. The outcome is known without any state vector work or randomness. k is unchanged.

OP_MEAS_DORMANT_RANDOM.summary: Random measurement of a dormant qubit.
OP_MEAS_DORMANT_RANDOM.detail: The qubit is in an equal superposition within the dormant subspace. The outcome is chosen by the RNG, and an algebraic phase is absorbed into gamma. No state vector work; k is unchanged.

OP_MEAS_ACTIVE_DIAGONAL.summary: Z-basis measurement of an active qubit (diagonal collapse).
OP_MEAS_ACTIVE_DIAGONAL.detail: Collapses the state vector along one active axis by keeping only the half consistent with the measurement outcome. Halves the array: k -> k-1. The outcome probability is computed from the squared amplitudes of each half.

OP_MEAS_ACTIVE_INTERFERE.summary: X-basis measurement of an active qubit (interference collapse).
OP_MEAS_ACTIVE_INTERFERE.detail: Collapses the state vector by folding (adding or subtracting) two halves together, depending on the measurement outcome. Halves the array: k -> k-1. This handles the case where the measured Pauli has off-diagonal terms.

OP_APPLY_PAULI.summary: Apply a full N-bit Pauli mask to the frame (classical feedback).
OP_APPLY_PAULI.detail: XORs a precomputed N-qubit Pauli bitmask from the constant pool into the Pauli frame vector P, conditioned on a prior measurement result. Implements classical feedback (e.g. Pauli corrections after teleportation).
OP_APPLY_PAULI.operands: cp_mask (constant pool index), condition (measurement record index)

OP_NOISE.detail: Rolls the RNG against a probability table from the constant pool. If triggered, applies a random Pauli (X, Y, or Z) to the frame. Models depolarizing, dephasing, and other Pauli noise channels.

OP_READOUT_NOISE.summary: Classical bit-flip noise on a measurement result.
OP_READOUT_NOISE.detail: Rolls the RNG against a readout error probability. If triggered, flips the classical measurement bit in the record. Models measurement readout errors separately from quantum noise.
OP_READOUT_NOISE.operands: cp_entry (constant pool readout noise entry index)

OP_DETECTOR.summary: Detector: parity check over measurement records.
OP_DETECTOR.detail: Computes the XOR parity of a set of measurement record entries (specified by a target list in the constant pool) and stores the result as a detector outcome. Used for quantum error correction syndrome extraction.
OP_DETECTOR.operands: cp_targets (constant pool target list index) -> det[detector_idx]

OP_POSTSELECT.summary: Post-selection: discard shot if parity check fails.
OP_POSTSELECT.detail: Same parity computation as OP_DETECTOR, but if the parity is nonzero the entire shot is discarded. Used for post-selected circuits where certain outcomes are conditioned upon.
OP_POSTSELECT.operands: cp_targets (constant pool target list index) -> det[detector_idx]

OP_OBSERVABLE.detail: Accumulates the XOR parity of measurement record entries into a logical observable. Used in error correction to track logical qubit values across multiple rounds of syndrome measurements.
OP_OBSERVABLE.operands: cp_targets (constant pool target list index) -> obs[observable_idx]

T_GATE.summary: T or T-dagger gate: pi/8 phase rotation on a Pauli product.
T_GATE.detail: The primary non-Clifford operation. Applies exp(i*pi/8 * P) where P is the Pauli product shown (e.g. +X0*Z1). T_DAG applies the conjugate rotation. These cannot be absorbed into the Heisenberg frame and are passed through to the back-end.

CLIFFORD_PHASE.summary: S or S-dagger gate: pi/4 phase rotation on a Pauli product.
CLIFFORD_PHASE.detail: Although S is a Clifford gate, when it appears in the HIR it means the front-end could not fully absorb it into the frame (e.g. it acts on a Pauli product that spans multiple qubits). S_DAG applies the inverse.

MEASURE.summary: Destructive measurement of a Pauli observable.
MEASURE.detail: Measures the Pauli product shown (e.g. +X0, -Z0*Z1) and stores the outcome in the measurement record. The Pauli is the effective observable after Heisenberg frame transformation -- it may differ from the original circuit's measurement basis.

CONDITIONAL_PAULI.summary: Classical feedback: apply Pauli correction conditioned on a measurement.
CONDITIONAL_PAULI.detail: If the referenced measurement record entry is 1, applies the shown Pauli product to the frame. Implements feed-forward operations like teleportation corrections and QEC feedback.

NOISE.detail: Represents a noise process (depolarizing, dephasing, etc.) that may randomly apply a Pauli operator. References a NoiseSite side-table with the channel probabilities.

READOUT_NOISE.summary: Classical bit-flip noise on a measurement result.
READOUT_NOISE.detail: Models measurement readout errors as a classical bit-flip probability applied to the measurement record after the quantum measurement.

DETECTOR.summary: Detector: parity check over measurement records.
DETECTOR.detail: Defines a parity check (XOR) over a set of measurement record entries. In error correction, detectors flag syndrome changes between rounds.

OBSERVABLE.detail: Accumulates measurement record parities into a logical observable for error correction tracking.
